text: CENTCOM Watch: US conducted self-defense strikes near Bandar Abbas on May 25 — hitting two IRGC boats caught laying mines at the Strait of Hormuz entrance and a SAM battery that had targeted US aircraft. Spokesman confirmed: 'ceasefire remains in effect.' This is the first US offensive action inside Iran since the April 8 ceasefire — 47 days. The timing is notable: it came while Iran's delegation was flying to Doha for MOU talks. US message: the blockade and self-defense rules remain fully active even during diplomacy.
verificationNote: CENTCOM strikes confirmed by NBC News and BNO News May 25. Spokesman Timothy Hawkins quote confirmed by NBC News. Ceasefire-in-effect statement per CENTCOM.

text: Lebanon Front Report: May 25 was the most intense day of IDF-Hezbollah fighting since the Lebanon ceasefire. Netanyahu issued a specific evening Telegram video ordering IDF to 'crush Hezbollah' — distinct from earlier daytime orders. IDF reached 70+ Hezbollah sites struck in 24 hours using 85+ munitions. Hezbollah responded with 30+ explosive drones; Mount Meron IDF base targeted. Lebanon death toll passed 3,185. Netanyahu was separately taken to Hadassah Ein Kerem hospital for dental work — his office says not emergency-related.
verificationNote: Netanyahu Telegram video confirmed by Al Jazeera, France 24, Al Arabiya May 25. IDF 70+ sites confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Lebanon toll per Al Jazeera live coverage.

text: Oil Market Signals: Brent crude fell to $96.31 (-5.91%) and WTI to $90.69 (-6.12%) on Memorial Day — lowest Brent since mid-April 2026. The 6% drop came on thin holiday volumes (US markets closed) driven entirely by Doha MOU optimism. Market is pricing a 60-70% probability of Hormuz reopening within 30-60 days. Analysts estimate: 100M barrels of stranded cargo would flood markets upon reopening; Brent could drop to $75-80 range within 3-6 months. Goldman Sachs revised target to $85 from $100.
verificationNote: Oil prices confirmed by TradingEconomics and Barchart. Market closed for Memorial Day per standard calendar. Peace deal optimism driver confirmed by BNN Bloomberg and The Hill energy reporting.

text: Iran Internet Tracker: Pezeshkian ordered international internet restoration after an 87-day blackout — the longest national internet shutdown on record globally (surpassing Myanmar's 2019 blackout). The shutdown began February 28, Day 1 of Operation Epic Fury, as part of emergency measures. The order has been issued but the Ministry of Communications has not given a timeline for reconnection. Analysts see this as a signal the Iranian government is preparing domestic expectations for a post-conflict normalization — part of the MOU diplomatic track.
verificationNote: Internet restoration order confirmed by Sunday Guardian Live and Haaretz May 25. Blackout duration of 87 days from Day 1 (Feb 28) per confirmed timeline.

text: Doha Tracks Desk: Iran's high-powered Doha delegation signals the deal is about economics, not just the ceasefire. Ghalibaf (Parliament Speaker) + Hemmati (Central Bank Governor) + Araghchi (FM) = a team designed to close the sanctions/oil/assets track, not the nuclear file. Iran's position: Phase 1 MOU covers Hormuz reopening + $25B frozen asset release + US naval blockade lifted. Nuclear issues deferred to Phase 2 framework. Iran's FM says uranium disposal is NOT in the preliminary agreement — directly contradicting US claims.
verificationNote: Doha delegation composition confirmed by Mehr News Agency and CBS News. Araghchi uranium 'not in initial agreement' confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 87 and Tribune India.

text: Austria Intel Watch: Austria's domestic intelligence service (DSN) has released its annual security report concluding Iran's nuclear weapons program is 'well-advanced' and that Iranian ballistic missiles are 'ready to carry nuclear warheads.' Iran's Foreign Ministry called the report 'fake information.' This assessment directly contradicts US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard's March 18 congressional testimony that 'Iran is not building a nuclear weapon.' The two official intelligence assessments are structurally incompatible — and they cannot both be correct.
verificationNote: Austrian DSN annual report confirmed by Iran International and Fox News May 25-26. Iran FM denial confirmed by Iran International. Gabbard testimony confirmed by CBS News March 18.

text: Oil Rebound Desk: Brent crude rebounded to $98.11/barrel (+$1.80) from the overnight Memorial Day low of $96.31 — the session low came on thin holiday volumes with US markets closed. WTI also recovered to ~$91.00. The rebound reflects: (1) CENTCOM self-defense strikes adding a geopolitical risk premium back to oil; (2) Austrian nuclear intel report raising nuclear deal uncertainty; (3) Iran's IRGC mine-laying activity at Hormuz entrance signaling Iran is not passively waiting. Markets are now pricing two competing scenarios: deal within 7-10 days (Brent $85-90 target) vs. deal failure + resumed strikes (Brent $110+ target).
verificationNote: Brent $98.11 and WTI $91.00 confirmed by Barchart and TradingEconomics May 26. Overnight low of $96.31 confirmed by TradingEconomics.

text: CENTCOM Aftermath Watch Day 88: The self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats + SAM site near Bandar Abbas on May 25 have not collapsed the Doha peace talks — Iran's delegation is still in Qatar this morning. This is significant: Iran chose to continue negotiations despite the first US strikes in Iran since the April 8 ceasefire. Two interpretations: (1) Iran views the strikes as proportionate and consistent with ceasefire terms, not worth blowing up the MOU process over; (2) Iran is too economically desperate to walk away from a deal. Either way, the ceasefire framework has so far survived its first serious test.
verificationNote: Iran delegation remaining in Doha confirmed by CBS News and Times of Israel liveblog May 26. CENTCOM strikes confirmed by NBC News.

text: Lebanon Day 88 Watch: Following Netanyahu's 'crush Hezbollah' order and the IDF's 70+ site strike campaign on May 25, Day 88 morning is relatively quiet on the Lebanon front. Hezbollah appears to be conducting a tactical pause — possibly to assess the IDF's new strike tempo before responding. Israel's evacuation orders for 10 Lebanese villages remain in effect. US officials reportedly told Israel they support continued operations but want calibrated escalation — not a Beirut bombing campaign. The Lebanon ceasefire is formally extended through early July but has been violated by both sides daily.
verificationNote: IDF 70+ sites and evacuation orders confirmed by Times of Israel and Al Jazeera May 25. Hezbollah pause noted by France 24 and Al Arabiya May 26.

text: Hormuz Day 85 Update: The IRGC mine-laying boats destroyed by CENTCOM were caught attempting to emplace new mines at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz — potentially in preparation for a renewed closure attempt if MOU talks collapsed. The Strait remains effectively closed; ~2 vessels per day transiting (vs. 140/day pre-war). The MOU framework under negotiation in Doha would have Iran clear existing mines in Phase 1 (30 days after signing). With an active IRGC mine-laying program exposed during active negotiations, the US side will press harder for concrete mine-removal guarantees before Hormuz reopens.
verificationNote: IRGC mine-laying confirmed by CENTCOM via NBC News and Stars & Stripes. Hormuz status per Wikipedia 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis.

text: Khamenei Watch Desk: Mojtaba Khamenei has broken near-silence with his most extensive public communication since succeeding his father — a written Hajj statement declaring 'Death to America and Death to Israel will continue to be the slogans of the Islamic Ummah' and vowing US military bases will leave the region. His '15 years' claim about Israel echoes his late father's 2015 '25 years' statement. The timing — issued simultaneously as Iran's delegation negotiates in Doha — is deliberate: signaling domestic hardliners Iran is not capitulating, even as pragmatic negotiators pursue a deal.
verificationNote: Statement confirmed by Bloomberg, Washington Times, Euronews, Jerusalem Post (May 26). Ali Khamenei '25 years' 2015 statement is historical record. Mojtaba succeeded as Supreme Leader Feb 28, 2026.

text: Trump Deal Watch Desk: Trump's Abraham Accords demand is a significant new complication to Iran MOU talks. He now requires Egypt, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia to 'simultaneously sign the Abraham Accords' as part of any Iran deal. This demand — not in the original ceasefire framework — effectively conditions Hormuz reopening on Palestinian normalization. Analysts note Qatar and Pakistan have zero domestic political space to sign the Accords right now. Republicans including Rubio are not publicly endorsing the demand. Camp David Cabinet meeting Wednesday may clarify scope.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by Times of Israel, CNN, Washington Post (May 26). Camp David meeting per Fox News and CNBC.

text: IDF Ground Ops Tracker: The IDF's Yellow Line crossing into Zawtar al-Sharqiya is the first confirmed breach of Israel's self-declared buffer zone in southern Lebanon since it was established. The Golani Brigade's weeklong raid north of the Litani claimed 45 targets and 15 Hezbollah fighters killed. Hezbollah countered with loitering munitions — claiming a Merkava tank destroyed at 08:45 local time. IDF also hit the road near Qaraoun Dam for the first time. Lebanese Health Ministry updated cumulative toll to 3,213 killed (9,737 wounded).
verificationNote: IDF Yellow Line expansion confirmed by Al-Monitor and Times of Israel. Golani raid per Times of Israel. Qaraoun Dam strike per Anadolu Agency. Lebanon toll per Al Jazeera.

text: Maritime Security Desk: The explosion aboard VLCC 'Olympic Life' ~60nm off Muscat, Oman raises the threat level in the broader Gulf of Oman maritime corridor. The incident occurred hours after CENTCOM sank IRGC mine-laying vessels near Bandar Abbas — suggesting either a mine from that operation drifted, or Iran conducted a retaliatory strike. UKMTO advisory is now in effect. Oil traders noted this event contributed to Brent crossing $100 intraday today — first time since early May.
verificationNote: Tanker explosion confirmed by gCaptain and Seatrade Maritime. UKMTO advisory confirmed. Brent $100+ intraday per CNBC.

text: Gaza Operations Watch: Netanyahu and Katz announced targeting Mohammed Odeh — Hamas's third military chief of the war — in a Gaza City strike. Odeh was appointed 11 days after his predecessor was killed. He served as Hamas intelligence chief on Oct 7. Israel has now publicly claimed targeting three consecutive Qassam Brigades commanders in the 2026 war. If confirmed, it represents a significant systematic effort to decapitate Hamas military leadership. Odeh's death not yet confirmed; 2 civilians killed in the struck building.
verificationNote: Netanyahu-Katz announcement confirmed by Times of Israel and Ynet May 26. Odeh's death not independently confirmed at time of reporting.

text: Oil PM Day 88 Update: Brent crude at $99.52/barrel (+24.7% vs pre-war $79.80), WTI at $93.89/barrel (+24.3% vs pre-war $75.51). Brent briefly crossed $100 intraday — driven by Mojtaba Khamenei's Hajj statement, Iran retaliation threats, and VLCC explosion off Oman. US gas at $4.491/gal (AAA, 4-year high). Day's narrative: ceasefire is holding by a thread — CENTCOM struck Iran, Iran threatened retaliation, IDF crossed Yellow Line, tanker hit off Oman — yet Iran's delegation remains in Doha. Rubio: 'a few days' until deal. Blockade Day 85.
verificationNote: Oil prices per CNBC May 26. Gas price per AAA newsroom. All events confirmed by cited news sources.

text: Iran Ceasefire Watch: The Iran FM's 'gross ceasefire violation' declaration marks the most formal protest since the April 8 ceasefire — but it's paired with Iran's delegation remaining in Doha. This is the pattern: Iran issues maximum rhetoric while continuing to negotiate. The IRGC's claimed MQ-9 shootdown fits the same template — maximum information operations pressure while preserving the diplomatic track. Key question: how long can Tehran maintain this dual-track before domestic hardliners force a real response?
verificationNote: Iran FM statement confirmed by Newsweek, Press TV, Al Jazeera (May 26). Doha delegation still present confirmed by CBS News. IRGC MQ-9 claim per Jerusalem Post (unconfirmed by CENTCOM).

text: Day 89 Oil Desk: Brent crude settles at ~$97.80/bbl (-1.7% from Day 88 PM high of $99.52). WTI at $93.96/bbl. The pullback from near-$100 levels reflects two countervailing forces: (1) Iran delegation still in Doha despite ceasefire violation rhetoric — market reads this as deal still alive; (2) But fresh CENTCOM strikes, IRGC MQ-9 claim, and Abraham Accords demand add uncertainty. Goldman Sachs maintains $85 Brent target conditional on Hormuz deal. If deal collapses: $115+ scenario. Gas steady at $4.491/gal. Hormuz Blockade Day 86.
verificationNote: Brent $97.80 and WTI $93.96 confirmed by CNBC, TradingEconomics (May 26-27). Gas $4.491 per AAA. Blockade Day 86 per calculation from Mar 3 start date.

text: Netanyahu Deal Watch: Netanyahu's 60 Minutes statement — 'Yes' regime change is possible, 'No' it's not guaranteed — combined with his insistence that any deal must 'eliminate the nuclear danger' including dismantling enrichment sites, sets up a collision with the emerging Trump-Iran framework. The US-Iran MOU draft does NOT include enrichment site dismantling — only a commitment to 'never pursue nuclear weapons.' IDF is simultaneously drawing up contingency plans in case an Iran deal forces it to halt Lebanon operations. This is the most significant US-Israel tension of the war.
verificationNote: Netanyahu 60 Minutes quote confirmed by Iran International, NPR (May 25). IDF Lebanon contingency plans per Times of Israel. MOU framework from Axios/CNN (May 24).

text: Abraham Accords Tracker: Trump's demand that Egypt, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain 'simultaneously sign the Abraham Accords' as part of any Iran deal is generating significant backlash. Qatar and Pakistan have zero domestic political space for normalization with Israel right now. Egypt and Jordan already have peace treaties with Israel but normalizing under these conditions would be domestically toxic. Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince MBS has been pursuing his own quiet normalization track for years — but on his timeline, not Trump's. This condition could be Trump's negotiating leverage or a genuine dealbreaker.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by Times of Israel, CNN, Washington Post (May 26). Camp David meeting Wednesday per Fox News. Rubio declining to endorse condition per ABC/NBC reporting.

text: Lebanon Day 89 Desk: IDF-Hezbollah Litani River clashes continue into Day 89 morning. Hezbollah's fiber-optic guided drone attacks on Israeli forces are the key tactical story — these drones are immune to GPS jamming (fiber-optic cable guided) and difficult to intercept. IDF has 11 soldiers killed since Lebanon ceasefire, mostly from these precision drone strikes. Israel's Yellow Line expansion into Zawtar al-Sharqiya signals intent to push Hezbollah north of the Litani permanently. Lebanon cumulative toll: 3,213 killed, 9,737 wounded. IDF drawing up contingency plans in case Iran deal forces halt to Lebanon operations.
verificationNote: Litani clashes confirmed by PBS NewsHour, Jerusalem Post (May 26). Fiber-optic drone capability confirmed by Times of Israel. Lebanon toll per Lebanese Health Ministry. IDF contingency plans per Times of Israel.

text: Iran Reconstitution Alert: US intelligence has confirmed to CNN that Iran is reconstituting its military capacity faster than projected — including restarting some drone production during the ceasefire. Chinese component transfers and Russian support are primary drivers. A US official: 'The Iranians have exceeded all timelines the IC had for reconstitution.' If accurate, this means any peace deal must account for Iran's ability to re-arm rapidly. The 60-day MOU framework may be too short to prevent a reset of Iran's offensive capacity. Austria's DSN: Iranian ballistic missiles 'ready to carry nuclear warheads.'
verificationNote: US intelligence assessment confirmed by CNN (May 21). Austrian DSN annual report confirmed by Iran International, Fox News (May 25-26). DEFCON Warning System assessment May 25.
